# Landing Page and Offer Factory

## The ad earns inspection; the page earns belief

A skeptical buyer rarely believes an ad. The page must carry proof.

## Page modules

| Module | Use when | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| Problem mirror | Cold/problem-aware | Show you understand the buyer's situation |
| Specific outcome | All | Make the promised transformation concrete |
| Product demo | Software | Move from claim to evidence |
| How it works | Skeptical/technical | Explain mechanism |
| Use cases | Multi-segment | Help buyer self-identify |
| Proof | Vendor-aware | Reviews, case studies, screenshots, metrics |
| Limitations | Skeptical/regulated | Build credibility by stating boundaries |
| Security/privacy | B2B/SaaS | Reduce data and compliance risk |
| Integrations | Ops-heavy buyers | Prove implementation fit |
| Pricing | Active buyers | Reduce uncertainty |
| Comparison | Crowded category | Clarify difference |
| FAQ | Objection-heavy | Remove final friction |
| CTA | All | Make next step low-risk |

## Page template for skeptical SaaS buyer

1. Headline: concrete job/outcome.
2. Subhead: mechanism + who it is for.
3. 60–120 second workflow demo.
4. Before/after example.
5. Permission/security preview.
6. Top three use cases.
7. Honest limitations.
8. Customer/review proof.
9. Pricing or trial expectation.
10. CTA: sandbox/trial/demo.

## Page-ad consistency rule

Every ad promise must appear in the first screen or first proof module of the landing page.

If an ad says “turn emails into Jira issues,” the page must show that exact transformation quickly.

## Offer testing

Test offers separately from copy.

- Same ad, different CTA.
- Same landing page, different offer.
- Same offer, different proof order.

Common SaaS offers:

- “Try without credit card.”
- “Use sample data first.”
- “Watch the workflow.”
- “Get an implementation estimate.”
- “Calculate your manual-work cost.”
- “See security and permissions.”
- “Book a technical demo.”

## Mindset

Landing pages are trust infrastructure. Do not let the ad make promises that the page cannot instantly substantiate.
